title: Wrong With Conviction: Why Confident Errors Evade Detection in Language Models — code, data, and reproducibility audit
description: Code, datasets, result files, and the claim-by-claim interactive reproducibility audit for the paper 'Wrong With Conviction: Why Confident Errors Evade Detection in Language Models.' Includes the participation-ratio theorem validation, the semantic-entropy double dissociation, the supervised commitment-band detector, the regime router, the subspace mechanism, and the integrated Governor.
